rulings.Case Title: ABDALLAH ELHANIA ET AL VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Disqualify Defense Law Firms
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, August 20, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFFs ABDALLAH ELHANIA, and ZAHRA ELMENJAOUI's Motion To Disqualify Defense Law Firms. Plaintiffs' motion to disqualify defense law firms is recharacterized per the order filed August 6, 2025 as seeking a ruling that defendants violated one or more discovery rules and, as recharacterized, the motion is denied. Plaintiffs have not shown that the subpoenas issued to the City of Sunnyvale or defense counsel's review of the documents produced the City of Sunnyvale contravened any provision of the Discovery Act or Welfare & Institutions Code 827(a), much less that defendants or their counsel willfully violated the Discovery Act or section 827(a). While there was a failure to provide actual timely service of the subpoenas on plaintiffs' counsel, that failure appears to be due to plaintiffs' counsel having different mailing and physical addresses and not due to any effort to make the subpoenas "secret" and unknown to plaintiffs. Nor have plaintiffs shown that they have standing to resist the disclosure of any of the records sought by or received by defendants from the subpoenas. Moreover, the records that were received by defendants were disclosed by presumably knowledgeable staff of the City of Sunnyvale who could have withheld disclosure if the records should not have been produced. Further, at least in part the disclosure of those records were later determined by the juvenile division of the Santa Clara Superior Court to be permissible. In all events, plaintiffs have not shown how they have been prejudiced by production of any of the records to defendants nor have they established any grounds for the extreme remedy of disqualifying defense counsel. This is a discovery order only and thus nothing in this ruling precludes plaintiffs from seeking, by way of motion in limine or in any other manner, an order from the trial judge precluding the admission of any of the records at trial. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEAH HICKEY VS. REBECCA HARRINGTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Defendant Rebecca Harringtons Motion For Protective Order To Limit Or Reduce Plaintiff Leah Hickeys Non Retained Experts
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, August 20, 2025, Line 2. DEFENDANT Rebecca Harrington's Motion For Protective Order To Limit Or Reduce Plaintiff Leah Hickeys Non Retained Experts. Defendant Rebecca Harrington's motion for a protective order to limit or reduce plaintiff Leah Hickey's non-retained experts is granted in part and, if there are any remaining disputes after a further meet and confer, the hearing is continued to August 25, 2025. A protective order will issue striking all portions of Ms. Hickey's expert disclosures purporting to reserve the right to call at trial any non-retained experts not identified by name. The protective order also requires that no later than the end of the business day on August 21, 2025 Ms. Hickey must identify each named non-retained expert who she does not expect to testify at trial if Ms. Harrington stipulates to the admissibility of medical records pertaining to that expert and, as to each such expert, identify the medical records that Ms. Hickey seeks a stipulation of admissibility. The parties are required to meet and confer beginning no later than 10am on August 22, 2025 in an effort to significantly reduce the number of non-retained experts that Ms. Hickey expects to call at trial. If by 3pm on August 22, 2025 the parties have not resolved all remaining disputes regarding the number of non-retained experts that Ms. Hickey intends to call at trial, no later than 4pm on August 22, 2025 each side must send an email to department 302 stating, without argument, her position(s) as to the remaining disputes and then appear in department 302 on August 25, 2025 at 9am. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC, IN ITS CAPACITY AS VS. BAUMAN LANDSCAPE & CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, August 20, 2025, Line 3. 1 - CROSS DEFENDANTs BAUMAN LANDSCAPE & CONSTRUCTION, INC., ANGELA BAUMAN, and MICHAEL BAUMAN DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendants Bauman Landscape & Construction, Inc., Michael Bauman and Angela Bauman's demurrer to the second, fourth and fifth causes of action in the cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant Bay Area Concrete LLC is sustained with ten calendar days leave to amend as to all three causes of action. The second cause of action for breach of the implied covenant fails to allege any breach of an implied term and is entirely duplicative of the breach of oral contract claim. The fourth cause of action for conversion fails to allege any wrongful act apart from the alleged breach of oral contract and thus, as currently alleged, is not cognizable per the economic loss rule. The fifth cause of action for fraud fails to allege either a claim for promissory fraud or fraud by concealment. A promissory fraud claim fails because no facts are alleged showing lack of intent to perform the alleged false promise. A claim for fraud by concealment fails because the allegedly concealed facts (the Baumans' failure to perform their contract with GSW and their contract with GSW forbid the Baumans' contract with BAC) are not material given BAC's allegation that the Baumans received all event tickets that they allegedly agreed to transfer to BAC. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN STATE WARRIORS, LLC, IN ITS CAPACITY AS VS. BAUMAN LANDSCAPE & CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, August 20, 2025, Line 3. 2 - CROSS DEFENDANTs BAUMAN LANDSCAPE & CONSTRUCTION, INC., ANGELA BAUMAN,and MICHAEL BAUMAN's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT. Cross-defendants Bauman Landscape & Construction, Inc., Michael Bauman and Angela Bauman's motion to strike the punitive damages allegations and prayer in the cross-complaint filed by cross-complainant Bay Area Concrete LLC is granted with ten calendar days leave to amend. As a result of the ruling on the Baumans' demurrer to the cross-complaint, there is currently no claim that supports punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: ALEXA WHITE VS. MARY LOUISE BEECROFT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft And Her Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, August 20, 2025, Line 5. PLAINTIFF ALEXA WHITE's Motion To Compel Further Responses By Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Mary Louise Beecroft And Her Attorney. Plaintiff Alexa White's motion to compel further responses by defendant Mary Beecroft to Ms. White's first set of document requests and for monetary sanctions is denied. Ms. White must pay $900 in monetary sanctions to Ms. Beecroft no later than September 23, 2025. This motion is a non-starter and should never have been filed. There is no basis to discover the trust documents and no privilege log is needed if no documents are not withheld on privilege grounds. Because the motion lacked substantial justification, Ms. White is required to pay the reasonable monetary sanctions requested by Ms. Beecroft. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) rulings.Case Title: RED OAK REALTY VS. VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, August 20, 2025, Line 7. DEFENDANTs VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC., JAMES NUNEMACHER, FRANK NOLAN, and PAMELA HOFFMAN DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action for tortious interference with contract in the third amended complaint filed by plaintiff Red Oak Realty is overruled. Red Oak sufficiently alleges that defendants committed the independently wrongful act of aiding and abetting Red Oak's agents' concealment of material information from Red Oak regarding listing agreements while they were in an agency relationship with Red Oak. rulings.Case Title: JENIFER HANKI VS. WAYMO LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Wednesday, August 20, 2025, Line 8. PLAINTIFF JENIFER HANKI DEMURRER to ANSWER to COMPLAINT. Plaintiff Jenifer Hanki's demurrer to the answer filed by defendants Waymo LLC is overruled in its entirety. The demurrer serves no useful purpose. The affirmative defenses alleged in Waymo's answer are commonly alleged in a personal injury case. While it is likely that many, if not most, of the affirmative defenses will be withdrawn before trial, at this early stage of the case it is neither improper nor outside the norms of Bay Area litigation for Waymo to have alleged them. If Ms. Hanki believes she needs more information about the affirmative defenses, she may propound discovery seeking that information. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.)
